snippets.title: A class with __init__, a method and __repr__
snippets.explanation: __init__ sets per-instance state via self; __repr__ gives a useful debug representation.

snippets.title: Instance vs class attributes (the shared-mutable trap)
snippets.explanation: Initialize mutable state in __init__; class-body mutables are shared across every instance.

snippets.title: Inheritance, super() and @property
snippets.explanation: super() reuses parent setup; @property exposes a computed value accessed like an attribute.

snippets.title: Duck typing: any object with run() is a tool
snippets.explanation: Python checks for the needed method, not the exact type — unrelated classes interoperate via a shared interface.
